opener: Spring is technically four days away but the crocuses in Wickford don't care about calendars. Neither do we. Here's what's worth your time this week in South County.

headliner.name: St. Patrick's Weekend Oyster & Stout Pairing
headliner.description: Six local stouts paired with six preparations of Point Judith oysters. They do this once a year and it sells out in 48 hours. If you're reading this and there are still tickets, stop reading and go buy them. We'll be here when you get back.

lineup.name: Acoustic Fridays: The Silks
lineup.description: Indie folk duo with a loyal local following. Get there early — the Mist doesn't do reservations and the parking lot tells you everything you need to know.

lineup.name: Wine Wednesday: Natural Wine Flight
lineup.description: Four natural wines, all under $15 a glass. The sommelier actually knows what she's talking about, which is more than we can say for most.

lineup.name: Narragansett Beach Cleanup
lineup.description: Earn your afternoon beer. Bags and gloves provided, coffee truck on-site. Actually a great way to meet people who aren't terrible.

lineup.name: Opening Night: 'The Humans' at Theatre by the Sea
lineup.description: Tony Award-winning family drama. If your in-laws are visiting this weekend, this is either the perfect outing or a terrible idea. You know which.

lineup.name: Saturday Night Karaoke
lineup.description: Liquid courage is $5 during happy hour. The song book is deep. Your dignity is optional.

lineup.name: Fantastic Umbrella Factory Spring Market
lineup.description: Local artisans, live animals, and the kind of weird-beautiful energy that makes you remember why you live here. Kids love the emus. Adults love the gift shop.

spotlight.name: Coast Guard House
spotlight.location_detail: Ocean Road, right on the seawall
spotlight.description: Yes, everyone knows it. But have you been since they revamped the raw bar menu? The tuna poke bowl is absurdly good for a place most people write off as touristy. Go on a weeknight, sit at the bar, watch the waves, and pretend you don't have anywhere to be tomorrow.

pvd_events.name: Providence Restaurant Week
pvd_events.description: Multi-course prix fixe menus across the city. Worth the drive for Oberlin, Bayberry Beer Hall, or Kleos.

deal.details: Valid Sundays through March 31. Mention 'South County Signal' when booking. Cannot be combined with other offers.

signal_noise.question: Best lobster roll in South County — and don't just say Aunt Carrie's because your parents did.
